name: Asset Inventory and Management
description: A complete, current OT asset inventory is the foundation of all other OT security controls. You cannot patch what you do not know you have. You cannot segment what you have not identified. You cannot monitor what you cannot see. OT asset inventories are consistently the first thing found inadequate in OT security assessments.

requirements.name: OT asset inventory establishment
requirements.description: Create and maintain a comprehensive inventory of all OT assets including PLCs, RTUs, HMIs, engineering workstations, historians, OT network devices, and all software deployed on OT systems.

technical.summary: OT asset inventory minimum fields: asset name, make/model, firmware/software version, IP address (if applicable), MAC address, physical location, zone assignment, owner, criticality rating, last security review date.
actions: Conduct initial OT asset discovery using passive network monitoring (deploy OT monitoring sensor on SPAN/TAP)
Supplement passive discovery with manual walkdown of OT areas for non-networked assets
Populate asset inventory database with all discovered assets
Include assets at remote sites (RTUs, field devices with network connectivity)
Update inventory within 5 business days of any asset addition, modification, or removal
Classify each asset by criticality: High (direct process control), Medium (supervisory/monitoring), Low (support systems)

governance.summary: Asset inventory owned by OT engineering. Security team maintains security attributes. Reviewed quarterly.
actions: Quarterly inventory review and reconciliation with physical walkdown
New asset commissioning process includes inventory update as mandatory step

evidenceItems: OT asset inventory (with minimum required fields)

slMapping.sl2: Comprehensive inventory; passive discovery used; quarterly review
slMapping.sl3: Automated inventory with passive monitoring; asset changes trigger automated update; alert on new/changed devices
slMapping.sl4: Continuous automated inventory; hardware identity verification; alert on any unregistered device

requirements.name: Asset criticality classification
requirements.description: Classify each OT asset by criticality based on the consequence of its compromise — to prioritise security controls, maintenance scheduling, and incident response.

technical.summary: Three-tier criticality: High (loss/compromise causes immediate safety, environmental, or major process impact), Medium (loss causes operational disruption requiring manual intervention), Low (loss causes minor inconvenience).
actions: Assess consequence of compromise for each asset class
Assign criticality tier (High/Medium/Low) to each asset
Map High-criticality assets to NACSA NCII designation scope
Apply enhanced security controls to High-criticality assets (higher SL-T, more frequent review)
Document criticality rationale for each High-criticality asset

evidenceItems: Mapping of High-criticality assets to NCII designation scope

slMapping.sl2: Criticality classification documented; controls differentiated by criticality
slMapping.sl3: Formal consequence analysis per IEC 62443-3-2; SL-T assigned per zone based on criticality

requirements.name: Software inventory and SBOM
requirements.description: Maintain an inventory of all software (OS, applications, firmware) on OT systems including version and patch level. Request Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) from vendors for critical OT products.

technical.summary: Software inventory per OT asset: OS version, installed applications with versions, firmware version. Enables rapid vulnerability assessment when new CVE is published.
actions: Record software and firmware versions for all OT assets in inventory
Update software inventory whenever patches are applied
Request SBOM from vendors for new OT product procurement (increasingly available; required for IEC 62443-4-1 certified products)
Use software inventory to rapid-assess exposure when high-severity CVEs are published
Flag OT assets running end-of-life OS (Windows XP, Windows 7 — still common in OT) for priority lifecycle replacement

evidenceItems: Software inventory (per asset: OS + applications + firmware + versions)

slMapping.sl3: Automated software inventory via monitoring platform; SBOM requested for critical products
